title: Bulk report - Week 4
summary:
Capesize
A marked improvement on the capes last week, with the headline west Australia/China route jumping up over US$1.00. Vessels were fixing around the US$5.60 level at the beginning of the week and now they are obtaining US$7.00 which was rumoured to have been fixed by FMG on cape tonnage loading 2 February. Saldanha to Qingdao was fixed at US$11.35 on capesize tonnage basis 11/20 February by NYK.

children.title: Capesize
children.summary: A marked improvement on the capes last week, with the headline west Australia/China route jumping up over US$1.00. Vessels were fixing around the US$5.60 level at the beginning of the week and now they are obtaining US$7.00 which was rumoured to have been fixed by FMG on cape tonnage loading 2 February.

children.title: Panamax
children.summary: The period market remained active with an ECO JMU type achieving US$14,500 for one year at the beginning of the week, however as the week progressed the focus shifted from one-year trades to shorter periods of four to six months. A decent number of fixtures were evident midweek and sentiment was positive, but on Thursday the spot market appeared to have a wobble as charterers began to challenge the status quo and hold back a little.

children.title: Supramax
children.summary: As the week progressed the supramax index lost ground mainly due to the Atlantic routes losing momentum, whilst the Asian routes generally stayed in more positive territory. Period activity however was again seen with a 63,000-dwt open east coast India fixing five to seven months trading redelivery worldwide at close to US$12,000.

children.title: Handysize
children.summary: Another pretty flat week overall in the Atlantic and whilst rates from the US Gulf started on a high, as the week progressed they traded sideways. From the Continent and Mediterranean rates eased slightly with a lack of fresh enquiry.
